title: During an Earthquake
steps: Drop, Cover, and Hold On — get under a sturdy table if possible.
Stay away from windows, mirrors, and heavy furniture that could fall.
If indoors, stay indoors until the shaking stops.
If outdoors, move to an open area away from buildings, trees, and power lines.
If driving, pull over away from bridges/overpasses and stay in the vehicle.
After shaking stops, check yourself and others for injuries before moving.

title: After an Earthquake
steps: Expect aftershocks — they can be strong enough to cause more damage.
Check for gas leaks, damaged wiring, and structural damage before re-entering buildings.
Use text messages instead of calls to keep phone lines free for emergencies.
Listen to official radio/alerts for evacuation instructions.
Use the Emergency Contacts and Location Sharing features to let family know you're safe.

title: Flood Safety
steps: Move immediately to higher ground if flash flooding is possible in your area.
Never walk, swim, or drive through flood waters — 15cm of moving water can knock you down.
Stay off bridges over fast-moving water.
If your vehicle is trapped in rising water, abandon it and move to higher ground if you can do so safely.
Avoid contact with flood water — it may be contaminated or electrically charged.

title: Fire Emergency
steps: Get out immediately — do not stop to gather belongings.
Stay low to the ground to avoid smoke inhalation.
Check doors for heat before opening; if hot, use another exit.
Once outside, stay outside and go to your designated meeting point.
Call emergency services as soon as you are safely away from the building.
